title: The Lark and Her Young Ones
lines: A Lark had built her nest in a field of grain.
When the grain was ripe and ready to be cut, the Lark was troubled about her young ones.
"We must fly away," she told them, "or we shall be in danger."
She went out to look for a new home, and finding none, returned to the nest.
The next morning she asked, "Has any one come to cut the grain?"
"No," replied the young ones, "we have heard the farmer tell his son to bring his friends to help cut the grain."
"Then we may stay a while longer," said the Lark.
She went out again, and again returned.
"No," said the young ones, "but the farmer said he would come with his father and do the work himself."
"Now we must go," cried the Lark; "when a man decides to do his own work, the thing is as good as done."
moral: Self-reliance is the road to success.
